cot_prefix: Okay, let's try to figure out when Anthony could have gone to the amusement park. Let me go through the information given step by step. First, Anthony woke up at 6am. Then, Leslie saw him reading at the library from 6am to 8am. So from 6 to 8, he's busy at the library. Then, Jennifer saw him at the gym working out from 8am to 9am. So that's 8 to 9 am. Then there's a gap until when? Let me check the next events. Anthony saw Anthony buying cookies at a bakery from 2pm to 9pm. Wait, that seems a bit long—seven hours. So from 2 to 9 pm, he's at the bakery. Then William saw Anthony buying a bike at the bike shop from 9pm to 10pm. And the amusement park is closed after 10pm. So let's map out Anthony's timeline: - 6am to 8am: Library - 8am to 9am: Gym - Then... nothing until 2pm? So from 9am to 2pm, there's no mention of Anthony's activities. Then from 2pm to 9pm, he's at the bakery. Then 9pm to 10pm at the bike shop. After 10pm, the park is closed. So the only time Anthony is unaccounted for is between 9am and 2pm. Because from 6-8am (library), 8-9am (gym), then 9am to 2pm is free, then 2-9pm (bakery), 9-10pm (bike shop), and after that closed. So the amusement park visit must have happened during the unaccounted time, which is 9am to 2pm. Let me check the options: Options: (A) 9pm to 10pm –
